intro: Diplostephium liratum, a type of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Diplostephium liratum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Diplostephium liratum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to relieve symptoms associated with colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
